metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students develop three-dimensional art skills including sculpture, modeling, and construction techniques. This course emphasizes form, space, material exploration, and the translation of ideas into three-dimensional work.
metadata.general_description: A course focused on three-dimensional art including sculpture, modeling, and form exploration.

big_ideas.statement: Three-dimensional art engages viewers in unique ways through form, space, and material presence.
big_ideas.elaboration: Sculptural and three-dimensional work invites viewers to move around and experience work from multiple perspectives, creating dynamic and immersive artistic experiences.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding form, space, and composition is essential to creating effective three-dimensional work.
big_ideas.elaboration: Artists working in three dimensions must consider volume, proportion, balance, and spatial relationships to create compelling artwork.

big_ideas.statement: Materials have expressive and conceptual significance in three-dimensional art.
big_ideas.elaboration: Artists choose materials intentionally to convey meaning, evoke responses, and explore material properties through creative practice.

big_ideas.statement: Three-dimensional art practices range from traditional sculpture to contemporary installation and conceptual approaches.
big_ideas.elaboration: Contemporary artists work across diverse techniques and materials, creating innovative three-dimensional work that challenges conventions and expands possibilities.

i_can_statements: I can create three-dimensional forms using various modeling techniques
I can work with clay, stone, wood, and other sculptural materials
I can understand additive and subtractive sculpture processes
I can develop technical skill in hand-based three-dimensional art
I can create forms with awareness of volume and proportion
I can understand how three-dimensional work occupies space
I can consider how viewers experience work from different viewpoints
I can use spatial relationships intentionally in my work

competencies.raw: Experiment with diverse materials and construction techniques
i_can_statements: I can work with traditional and non-traditional materials creatively
I can explore material properties and possibilities
I can use construction and assembly techniques
I can combine materials in innovative ways
I can create a series of related three-dimensional works
I can develop consistent themes or interests in my sculpture
I can refine my approach through repeated practice
I can build a cohesive body of three-dimensional work

competencies.raw: Analyze three-dimensional art and sculptural forms
i_can_statements: I can identify and describe formal qualities of three-dimensional work
I can analyze how form and space create meaning
I can understand sculptural techniques and processes
I can evaluate the effectiveness of three-dimensional artwork

competencies.raw: Understand material choices and their significance
i_can_statements: I can explain why artists choose particular materials
I can analyze how material affects meaning and impact
I can understand material properties and limitations
I can make intentional material choices in my work

competencies.raw: Reflect on personal three-dimensional artistic development
i_can_statements: I can evaluate my three-dimensional work and technical growth
I can identify my sculptural interests and artistic voice
I can reflect on how ideas translate into form
I can set goals for developing as a three-dimensional artist

competencies.raw: Connect three-dimensional work to broader artistic contexts
i_can_statements: I can understand sculpture traditions across history and cultures
I can analyze contemporary three-dimensional art practices
I can place personal work within broader artistic conversations
I can appreciate diverse approaches to three-dimensional art
I can photograph three-dimensional work from multiple angles
I can create video documentation of three-dimensional pieces
I can maintain detailed records of my sculptural work
I can create professional documentation of my three-dimensional art
I can display three-dimensional work effectively
I can consider how space and installation affect viewer experience
I can present my work in exhibitions or presentations
I can discuss my sculptural work with audiences

competencies.raw: Communicate artistic intent and concepts in three-dimensional work
i_can_statements: I can articulate the ideas and meanings in my three-dimensional work
I can explain my artistic choices and conceptual approach
I can write about sculpture and three-dimensional art
I can share my artistic vision with others

competencies.raw: Communicate through three-dimensional form and space
i_can_statements: I can express ideas through three-dimensional forms
I can use sculpture to explore meaningful concepts
I can create work that invites viewer engagement
I can develop my voice as a three-dimensional artist

competencies.raw: Connect three-dimensional work to other art forms and disciplines
i_can_statements: I can understand relationships between sculpture and other visual arts
I can integrate two-dimensional and three-dimensional approaches
I can explore connections between sculpture and performance or installation
I can work across disciplines in my artistic practice

competencies.raw: Explore diverse sculptural traditions and contemporary practices
i_can_statements: I can study sculptural traditions from diverse cultures and periods
I can understand contemporary installation and site-specific art
I can adapt practices from diverse traditions to my own work
I can develop personal voice in three-dimensional art

competencies.raw: Engage with three-dimensional art in social and environmental contexts
i_can_statements: I can create work exploring social or cultural themes
I can address environmental concerns through sculpture
I can use three-dimensional art to spark community conversation
I can engage meaningfully with the world through sculpture

competencies.raw: Build awareness of sculpture careers and professional sculpture practice
i_can_statements: I can understand careers in sculpture and three-dimensional art
I can develop a portfolio of three-dimensional work
I can explore pathways to professional sculpture practice
I can build professional presence as a sculptor or three-dimensional artist

areas.name: Sculptural Traditions and Contemporary Practice
topics: History of sculpture across cultures and periods
